Title: S1. AHC001 / AtCoder Ad

Problem:
Place many non-overlapping axis-aligned ad rectangles in a 10000 x 10000 square so each advertiser gets a rectangle containing its requested point and with area close to its requested target area.

Return format:

Question ID: coding-S1-ahc001-atcoder-ad
Title: S1. AHC001 / AtCoder Ad

Problem:
Place many non-overlapping axis-aligned ad rectangles in a 10000 x 10000 square so each advertiser gets a rectangle containing its requested point and with area close to its requested target area.

Grading target:
Continuous score from advertiser satisfaction.
Higher is better.

Return format:
FINAL_ANSWER: <your answer>
JUSTIFICATION: <brief justification>

Node started: Technical Specification: Geometric Rectangle Packing and Area Optimization. Problem Overview: Perform a constrained optimization to place a set of non-overlapping, axis-aligned rectangles within a bounded 2D plane, where each rectangle must contain a specific target point and approximate a target area. 1. Mathematical Problem Statement: Let S be the bounding square defined by the domain D = {(x, y) in R^2 | 0 <= x <= 10000, 0 <= y <= 10000}. Given a set of N advertisers A = {a1, a2, ..., an}, where each advertiser ai is defined by a tuple (Pi, Ti): Pi = (xi, yi): The required coordinate point such that Pi is in Ri. Ti: The requested target area. Objective: Maximize the total advertiser satisfaction Z, where satisfaction is a function of the deviation between the actual area Area(Ri) and the target area Ti, subject to geometric constraints. Maximize Z = sum_{i=1}^{n} f(Area(Ri), Ti). (Note: The specific decay function f is continuous; typically, minimizing |Area(Ri) - Ti| or maximizing -|Area(Ri) - Ti|/Ti is implied). 2. Constraints: Hard Constraints (Mandatory): Containment: For every i, the rectangle Ri = [xmin,i, xmax,i] x [ymin,i, ymax,i] must satisfy 0 <= xmin,i < xmax,i <= 10000 and 0 <= ymin,i < ymax,i <= 10000. Point Inclusion: For every i, xmin,i <= xi <= xmax,i and ymin,i <= yi <= ymax,i. Non-Overlapping: For any two distinct rectangles Ri and Rj, Ri intersection Rj = empty set (the intersection must have an area of zero). Soft Constraints (Optimization Targets): Area Fidelity: Minimize the discrepancy between Area(Ri) and Ti for all i. 3. Required Output Format: The solution must be returned in the following structure: FINAL_ANSWER: <List of rectangles, where each rectangle is defined by (x_min, y_min, x_max, y_max)> JUSTIFICATION: <Brief technical explanation of the placement strategy used to balance area targets and non-overlap constraints> 4.
